recipes.title: Griot
recipes.description: Haitian fried pork shoulder marinated in epis and sour orange, then braised and crisped, served with pikliz.

ingredients.name: sour orange juice (or 50/50 lime and orange)

steps: Wash pork with sour orange and rub with epis, garlic, thyme, scotch bonnet, salt and pepper; marinate overnight.
Place the marinated pork and its juices in a heavy pot with a little water, cover and braise 1 hour until tender.
Uncover and cook off all liquid; reserve the pan juices for sauce.
Heat oil to 180°C and fry the pork in batches until deeply caramelised on all sides.
Drain on paper, drizzle with reduced pan juices and serve with pikliz, banan peze and rice.

recipes.title: Diri ak Pwa
recipes.description: Haitian rice and beans (red kidney beans) cooked together with epis, thyme and coconut milk for a creamy finish.

ingredients.name: dried red kidney beans, soaked overnight

steps: Cook soaked beans in water until tender, about 60 minutes; reserve beans and cooking liquid.
Sauté garlic and epis in oil, add the beans and a little of their liquid.
Add coconut milk, thyme, whole scotch bonnet, salt and enough water to reach 750 ml total liquid.
Stir in the rice, bring to a simmer, cover and cook on low for 25 minutes.
Remove the scotch bonnet (intact) and thyme stems; fluff and serve.

recipes.title: Soup Joumou
recipes.description: Haitian Independence Day pumpkin and beef soup with pasta and root vegetables, a UNESCO heritage dish.
steps: Marinate beef in epis, lime juice, salt and pepper for at least 1 hour.
Brown beef in a pot, add water and simmer 60 minutes until tender.
Boil the pumpkin in salted water until soft, then blend smooth and add to the beef pot.
Add potatoes, carrots, turnip, celery and the whole scotch bonnet; simmer 20 minutes.
Add cabbage and pasta and cook 10 minutes more.
Finish with thyme and a squeeze of lime, remove the chilli, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Pikliz
recipes.description: Haitian fiery pickled cabbage, carrot and scotch bonnet relish, the indispensable companion to fried foods.
steps: Pack cabbage, carrot, shallot, bell pepper, scotch bonnet, garlic and cloves into a clean glass jar.
Stir salt into the vinegar and lime juice until dissolved.
Pour the brine over the vegetables to cover.
Seal and refrigerate at least 3 days for flavours to develop; pikliz keeps for months.

recipes.title: Akra
recipes.description: Haitian malanga (taro root) fritters seasoned with garlic, scotch bonnet and parsley, fried until crisp.
steps: Finely grate the malanga to a thick, slightly stringy paste.
Stir in garlic, scotch bonnet, shallot, parsley, egg, salt and pepper.
Heat oil to 180°C.
Drop heaping tablespoons of batter into the oil and fry 3-4 minutes until deep golden and crisp.
Drain on paper and serve immediately with pikliz.

recipes.title: Lambi
recipes.description: Haitian conch braised in tomato, onion, bell pepper and scotch bonnet sauce, a coastal speciality.
steps: Pound the conch with a meat mallet to tenderise, then wash with lime juice.
Boil in salted water for 30-40 minutes until tender, then slice into bite pieces.
Sauté onion, bell pepper and garlic in oil, add tomato paste and tomatoes.
Stir in epis, thyme and the whole scotch bonnet; simmer 10 minutes.
Add the conch and a cup of its cooking liquid; simmer 15 minutes until the sauce coats the conch.
Adjust seasoning and serve with rice.

recipes.title: Tassot
recipes.description: Haitian crispy fried beef, marinated in epis and citrus, twice-cooked until deeply browned and chewy-crisp.

ingredients.name: beef chuck or top round, cubed

steps: Wash beef with lime juice and rub with epis, garlic, thyme, scotch bonnet, salt and pepper; marinate 4 hours.
Place beef and its marinade in a pot with a little water; cover and braise 60 minutes until tender.
Uncover and cook off all liquid; the beef should be dry but moist inside.
Heat oil to 180°C and fry the beef in batches 5-6 minutes until deeply browned on all sides.
Drain on paper and serve with pikliz, banan peze and rice.

recipes.title: Diri Djon Djon
recipes.description: Haitian black-rice dish stained and perfumed by djon djon mushrooms, often served with shrimp or lima beans.
steps: Steep djon djon in hot water for 20 minutes, then strain and reserve the inky liquid (discard or chop the mushrooms).
Sauté shallot, garlic and epis in oil, then add lima beans and rinsed rice and toast 1 minute.
Pour in the strained djon djon water, add salt, thyme and the whole scotch bonnet.
Bring to a boil, cover, lower heat and cook 25 minutes.
Rest 10 minutes off the heat, fluff with a fork and serve.

recipes.title: Poul Nan Sòs
recipes.description: Haitian stewed chicken in a tomato, bell pepper and onion sauce seasoned with epis, the everyday Sunday plate.
steps: Wash chicken with lime juice and water and pat dry, then rub with epis, salt and pepper; marinate 1 hour.
Brown chicken in oil on all sides; remove.
Sauté onion, bell pepper and garlic in the same pot, then add tomato paste and tomatoes.
Return the chicken, add water, thyme and the whole scotch bonnet; cover and simmer 30 minutes.
Uncover and reduce the sauce until thick and glossy; remove the chilli intact and serve with rice.
